Name: Design database schema
Description: Create the relational schema for users, vendors, admins, products, images, permissions, product views, orders, bookmarks, and purchase history.

Name: Implement real-time socket communication
Description: Set up a SignalR hub (or equivalent) on the .Net Core backend and a JavaScript client to broadcast events such as product approval, purchase notifications, and permission changes.

Name: user registration
Description: Provide a frontend form for vendors to submit registration data and a backend endpoint that stores the pending vendor account.

Name: Admin approve vendor registration
Description: Create a backend operation and admin UI to change a pending vendor account status to approved.

Name: Admin reject vendor registration
Description: Create a backend operation and admin UI to change a pending vendor account status to rejected.

Name: user login
Description: Implement a login page for Admin, Vendor, and Customer and a backend authentication service that issues JWT tokens.

Name: user logout
Description: Provide a logout mechanism that clears the authentication token on the client and invalidates it on the server if needed.

Name: Enable vendor permission
Description: Backend service and admin UI to turn on a specific permission (feature) for a vendor role or an individual vendor.

Name: Disable vendor permission
Description: Backend service and admin UI to turn off a specific permission (feature) for a vendor role or an individual vendor.

Name: Vendor create product post
Description: Frontend form for vendors to enter product details (title, description, price, images, units, etc.) and backend endpoint to store the new product as pending.

Name: Vendor list/manage own products
Description: Display a vendor’s products with pagination and provide UI controls for edit/delete; backend returns the vendor’s product list.

Name: Vendor update product details
Description: Backend endpoint and frontend form to modify any mutable fields of an existing product.

Name: Vendor delete product
Description: Backend operation and UI button that removes a product record permanently.

Name: Vendor update product inventory
Description: Backend service and UI control to change the Number of available units for a product.

Name: Admin accept product post
Description: Backend operation and admin UI to mark a vendor-submitted product as approved so it becomes visible to customers.

Name: Admin reject product post
Description: Backend operation and admin UI to mark a vendor-submitted product as rejected, preventing it from being shown.

Name: Admin enable auto-approval for vendor
Description: Backend setting and admin UI that automatically approves all future product posts from a selected vendor.

Name: Customer view all posted products
Description: Frontend page that fetches and displays every approved product; backend endpoint returns the product list.

Name: Customer search products
Description: Implement search UI with filters for category and price range and a backend query that returns matching products.

Name: Record product view count
Description: Backend increment of the Number of viewers each time a customer opens a product detail page; frontend triggers the increment.

Name: Customer purchase product
Description: Frontend checkout flow and backend order processing that validates stock, decrements inventory, creates an order record, and prevents purchase when units are zero.

Name: Customer save (bookmark) product post
Description: Frontend button to mark a product as saved and backend endpoint to store the bookmark for the customer.

Name: Vendor view purchase history for a product
Description: Backend query and frontend table that shows all customers who bought a specific product and their purchase details.

Name: Overdue Notifications
Description: Write a PL/SQL procedure that identifies students with overdue books and logs notification details into the NotificationLogs table. Check for books overdue more than 7 days and include student ID, book ID, overdue days, and notification date.

Name: Dynamic Late Fee Calculation
Description: Write a PL/SQL function that calculates late fees based on overdue days and book type. Takes borrow_record_id as input, calculates fee ($1/day for regular books, $2/day for reference books), saves to Penalties table, and returns the fee.

Name: Borrowing Validation Trigger
Description: Create a PL/SQL trigger that prevents a student from borrowing a book if they have overdue books or reached the borrowing limit (e.g., 3 books). Raises an exception to prevent invalid borrowing.

Name: Audit Trail for Borrowing Records
Description: Create BEFORE UPDATE and BEFORE DELETE triggers to log modifications or deletions of borrowing records into AuditTrail table. Capture table name, operation type, old data, new data, and timestamp.

Name: Borrowing History Report
Description: Write a PL/SQL cursor that retrieves a student’s borrowing history including overdue books and penalties. Input: student_id. Output: book title, borrow date, return date, status, and associated penalties.

Name: Safe Return Process with Transactions
Description: Write a PL/SQL block to handle returning multiple books in one transaction. Check for overdue penalties, ROLLBACK on errors, update BorrowingRecords and Penalties tables if successful.

Name: Books Availability Report
Description: Write a PL/SQL block to generate a detailed report of book availability. Include current status (available, borrowed, overdue), borrower info if borrowed, overdue days, and display in readable format using DBMS_OUTPUT.

Name: Automated Suspension
Description: Create a PL/SQL procedure to automatically suspend students whose unpaid penalties exceed a threshold (e.g., $50). Updates membership_status to 'suspended' in Students table.

Name: User Management and Privileges
Description: Create a Manager user to grant two users roles. User 1 creates Books and BookTypes tables. User 2 inserts 5 rows of book details and types into tables.

Name: Blocker-Waiting Situation
Description: Demonstrate a blocker-waiting scenario with two transactions. User 1 locks BorrowingRecords while updating, User 2 tries to insert penalty for the same record. Display how the session waits.

Name: Identifying Blocker and Waiting Sessions
Description: Identify sessions in blocker-waiting scenario using SID and SERIAL# for blocker and waiting sessions. Display session details and explain resolution.

Name: Deadlock Demonstration
Description: Demonstrate deadlock scenario: User 1 updates Books while trying to lock BorrowingRecords, User 2 updates BorrowingRecords while trying to lock Books. Show handling using rollback mechanisms.

Name: Build positional index using MapReduce
Description: Implement a MapReduce job in Java that reads the dataset and constructs a positional inverted index with term positions within each document.

Name: Display positional index
Description: Format and display each term with its document IDs and positions in the required structure <term doc1: pos1, pos2; doc2: pos1, pos2; ...>.

Name: Compute term frequency (TF)
Description: Calculate the frequency of each term in each document using the positional index output and display TF values.

Name: Compute inverse document frequency (IDF)
Description: Compute IDF for each term based on the number of documents containing the term and display results.

Name: Compute TF-IDF matrix
Description: Compute TF-IDF values for each term-document pair and display the resulting matrix.

Name: Implement phrase query with Boolean operators
Description: Build a query system that supports phrase queries with AND and AND NOT operators using the positional index.

Name: Compute similarity between query and documents
Description: Compute cosine similarity between query and matched documents using TF-IDF vectors.

Name: Rank documents
Description: Sort documents based on similarity scores in descending order.

Name: Return relevant documents
Description: Return ranked list of relevant documents as final output.
